items.briefing:
Good news for Australian organisations: overall cybercrime risk in Australia is reportedly declining, but pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses (SMBs), which are increasingly being targeted. One active threat involves ransomware attackers impersonating Interpol to trick small businesses into paying fake fines — if your organisation receives an unexpected email or message claiming to be from a law enforcement agency demanding payment, treat it as a scam.

Apple has also changed how it delivers security patches, which may affect how quickly your Apple devices receive fixes — ensure automatic updates are enabled on all work devices.

**Tip:** If you receive any urgent message claiming to be from Interpol or police demanding money, do not pay — report it to Scamwatch immediately.

featured.title: Yahoo Boys, a persistent menace and one of the longest running online crime cultures
featured.quote: Yahoo Boys represent evolved cybercrime threat using AI deepfakes, directly targeting Australian businesses and individuals.
